  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION

 27â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl

 29â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 30â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 31â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings

 33â†’
 34â†’/**
 35â†’ * Ensures multiple annotations are not on the same line as the annotated declaration. Also ensures that annotations
 36â†’ * with parameters are placed on separate lines.
 37â†’ *
 38â†’ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules. Moved since
 41â†’ */
 42â†’public class AnnotationRule : Rule("annotation") {
 43â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 44â†’        node: ASTNode,
 45â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 46â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 47â†’    ) {
 48â†’        when (node.elementType) {
 49â†’            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
 50â†’                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 51â†’            }
 52â†’            ANNOTATION_ENTRY ->
 53â†’                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 54â†’        }
 55â†’    }
 56â†’
 57â†’    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 58â†’        node: ASTNode,
 59â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 60â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 61â†’    ) {
 62â†’        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 63â†’
 64â†’        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 65â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 66â†’            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 67â†’            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 68â†’            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 69â†’        ) {
 70â†’            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 71â†’        }
 72â†’
 73â†’        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 74â†’            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 75â†’        ) {
 76â†’            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 77â†’        }
 78â†’
 79â†’        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 80â†’            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 81â†’            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 82â†’        ) {
 83â†’            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 84â†’        }
 85â†’    }
 86â†’
 87â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 88â†’        node: ASTNode,
 89â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 90â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 91â†’    ) {
 92â†’        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 93â†’            emit(
 94â†’                node.startOffset,
 95â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
 96â†’                true,
 97â†’            )
 98â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
 99â†’                node
100â†’                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
101â†’                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
102â†’            }
103â†’        }
104â†’
105â†’        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
106â†’            emit(
107â†’                node.startOffset,
108â†’                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
109â†’                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
110â†’                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
111â†’                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
112â†’                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
113â†’                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
114â†’            )
115â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
116â†’                node
117â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
118â†’                    .nextLeaf()
119â†’                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
120â†’                    ?.let {
121â†’                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
122â†’                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
123â†’                        } else {
124â†’                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
125â†’                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
126â†’                            )
127â†’                        }
128â†’                    }
129â†’            }
130â†’        }
131â†’    }
132â†’
133â†’    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
134â†’        node: ASTNode,
135â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
136â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
137â†’    ) {
138â†’        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
139â†’            val noAnnotationWithParameters =
140â†’                node
141â†’                    .siblings(forward = false)
142â†’                    .none { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
143â†’            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
144â†’                emit(
145â†’                    node.treeParent.startOffset,
146â†’                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
147â†’                    true,
148â†’                )
149â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
150â†’                    node
151â†’                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
152â†’                        .nextCodeLeaf()
153â†’                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
154â†’                }
155â†’            }
156â†’        }
157â†’    }
158â†’
159â†’    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
160â†’        node: ASTNode,
161â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
162â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
163â†’    ) {
164â†’        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
165â†’            node
166â†’                .siblings(forward = true)
167â†’                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
168â†’        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =
169â†’            node
170â†’                .siblings(forward = false)
171â†’                .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172â†’        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
173â†’            emit(
174â†’                node.startOffset,
175â†’                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
176â†’                true,
177â†’            )
178â†’            if (autoCorrect) {
179â†’                node
180â†’                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
181â†’                    .nextLeaf()
182â†’                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
183â†’            }
184â†’        }
185â†’    }
186â†’
187â†’    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() =
188â†’        getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
189â†’
190â†’    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
191â†’        psi
192â†’            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
193â†’            ?.useSiteTarget
194â†’            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
195â†’
196â†’    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() =
197â†’        getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
198â†’
199â†’    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
200â†’        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
201â†’            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
202â†’
203â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
204â†’        treeParent
205â†’            .children()
206â†’            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
207â†’            .let { it == this }
208â†’
209â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
210â†’        siblings(forward = false).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
211â†’
212â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
213â†’        siblings(forward = false)
214â†’            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
215â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
216â†’
217â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
218â†’        siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
219â†’
220â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
221â†’        siblings(forward = true)
222â†’            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
223â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
224â†’
225â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() =
226â†’        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
227â†’            .leaves(forward = true)
228â†’            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() }
229â†’            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
230â†’
231â†’    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
232â†’        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
233â†’            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
234â†’        } else {
235â†’            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
236â†’        }
237â†’
238â†’    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
239â†’        node: ASTNode,
240â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
241â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
242â†’    ) {
243â†’        node
244â†’            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
245â†’            .nextCodeLeaf()
246â†’            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
247â†’                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
248â†’
249â†’                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
250â†’                    emit(
251â†’                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
252â†’                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
253â†’                        true,
254â†’                    )
255â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
256â†’                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
257â†’                    }
258â†’                }
259â†’            }
260â†’    }
261â†’
262â†’    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
263â†’        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
264â†’        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
265â†’        // text in the file
266â†’        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
267â†’        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
268â†’            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
269â†’            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
270â†’        } else {
271â†’            newLineWithIndent
272â†’        }
273â†’    }
274â†’}

1â†’# Changelog
 2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4â†’
 5â†’## Unreleased
 6â†’
 7â†’### Added
 8â†’
 9â†’### Removed
10â†’
11â†’### Fixed
12â†’* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
13â†’* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
14â†’* 
15â†’
16â†’### Changed
17â†’
18â†’## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
19â†’
20â†’### Added
21â†’
22â†’### Removed
23â†’
24â†’### Fixed
25â†’
26â†’* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
27â†’* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
28â†’* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
29â†’* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
30â†’* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
31â†’* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))
32â†’
33â†’### Changed
34â†’
35â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.0` and Kotlin version to `1.8.0`.
36â†’
37â†’## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
38â†’
39â†’### Indent rule
40â†’
41â†’The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
42â†’
43â†’### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
44â†’
45â†’In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
46â†’
47â†’The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
48â†’
49â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers
50â†’

Now I understand the structure. In Kotlin, `@[JvmStatic Provides]` is an `ANNOTATION` node that contains multiple `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` children. The current code only visits `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes. 

The issue #1765 is about the array syntax annotation `@[...]` being on the same line as the annotated construct. Currently, the rule handles `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` but doesn't handle the `ANNOTATION` node (the `@[...]` syntax) properly when it's on the same line as the annotated construct.

content.text:
Let me understand the issue better. With `@[JvmStatic Provides]`, this is the Kotlin array syntax for annotations. The AST for this would be:

- `ANNOTATION` node containing `@`, `[`, multiple `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes, `]`

The problem is that when `@[...]` is on the same line as the annotated construct, the rule doesn't force it onto a separate line. This is because:

1. The rule only visits `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes
2. When an `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` is inside an `ANNOTATION` (array syntax), the check at line 79 explicitly skips the "multiple annotations on same line" check: `if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&`
3. The individual `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes inside `@[...]` don't have `isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()` in the same way since their siblings are within the `ANNOTATION` node

The fix should add handling for the `ANNOTATION` element type. When we encounter an `ANNOTATION` node that is on the same line as the annotated construct, we should force it onto a separate line.

So the parent of `ANNOTATION` nodes is a `MODIFIER_LIST`. The structure for `@[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo()` would be:
```
MODIFIER_LIST
  ANNOTATION (@[...])
    ANNOTATION_ENTRY (JvmStatic)
    ANNOTATION_ENTRY (Provides)
  WHITE_SPACE
FUN
```

Now, the `ANNOTATION` node can be followed by another annotation entry, or be followed by the annotated construct. The key insight from the issue description is: **always force `@[...]` annotations onto separate lines**.

This means if `@[...]` is on the same line as the annotated construct, we should put it on a separate line. Let me now implement the fix:

All existing tests pass. Now let me also check the existing test that specifically tests `@[JvmStatic Provides]` to make sure it still passes correctly. It should, since that test has the annotation on a separate line already.

Now let me think about what the issue really is. Looking at the existing test at line 543-550:

```kotlin
@[JvmStatic Provides]
fun foo() = 42
```

This has the annotation already on a separate line, so it has no violations. The issue #1765 would be about when the array-syntax annotation is on the same line:

```kotlin
@[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo() = 42
```

My new `visitAnnotation` method handles this case by checking if the `ANNOTATION` node is on the same line as the annotated construct and emitting a violation if so.

Let me also think about edge cases:
1. `@[JvmStatic Provides]` already on separate line - should be fine (not on same line)
2. `@[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo()` - should emit violation (on same line)
3. `@[JvmStatic Provides]fun foo()` - should emit violation (no space, but still on same line)

The `isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()` function checks the leaves after the last child leaf of the node, looking for whitespace with newline. This should work correctly for all these cases.

Let me also think about whether the existing test for `@[JvmStatic Provides]` on a separate line would be affected. Looking at the test:

```kotlin
@[JvmStatic Provides]
fun foo() = 42
```

The `isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()` method will find that there IS a newline in the whitespace between `]` and `fun`, so it returns `false` (it finds a whitespace with newline), and no violation is emitted. 

Now let me also think about the `visitAnnotationEntry` interactions. When `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes inside `@[...]` are visited, they have `treeParent.elementType == ANNOTATION`. Let me trace through:

1. For `@[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo()`:
   - `visitAnnotation` is called on the `ANNOTATION` node â†’ emits violation (on same line as annotated construct)
   - `visitAnnotationEntry` is called on `JvmStatic` (inside the ANNOTATION):
     - Not `isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList()` â†’ skip first check
     - `isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()` is true (Provides follows), `isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()` is true â†’ enters `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`
     - But wait... inside `@[...]`, the annotation entries are within the brackets and their siblings would be other annotation entries within the ANNOTATION node.

Hmm, this is a concern. Let me think about whether `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes inside `@[...]` would also trigger violations...

The `siblings()` function on a node returns siblings at the same tree level. For `@[JvmStatic Provides]`, the `JvmStatic` annotation entry's siblings would include `Provides` (both are children of the `ANNOTATION` node). So:

- `JvmStatic.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()` â†’ true (Provides is a sibling)
- `JvmStatic.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()` â†’ true (they're on the same line)

This would trigger `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine` which emits "Annotation must be placed on separate line". But this behavior is for entries within the `@[...]` syntax where entries are naturally on the same line. This is the EXISTING behavior though â€” let me check.

Looking at the existing test at line 543-550:
```kotlin
@[JvmStatic Provides]
fun foo() = 42
```
This test claims `hasNoLintViolations()`. This means that currently, the `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes inside `@[...]` do NOT trigger the "placed on separate line" check. Let me trace through again more carefully...

For `JvmStatic` inside `@[JvmStatic Provides]`:
- `isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()` returns true
- `isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()` returns true

So the condition `(node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry())` is true.

And `(node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())` â€” for JvmStatic, `isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()` is false (it's the first entry).

So the first condition IS true. We enter `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`.

In `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`:
- `isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList`: checks if any sibling annotation entry has a value argument list â†’ false (neither JvmStatic nor Provides has params)
- `isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine`: checks if any preceding sibling is whitespace with newline â†’ false (JvmStatic is the first entry)

Since both are false, no violation is emitted. This explains why the existing test passes.

Now for `Provides` inside `@[JvmStatic Provides]`:
- `isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()` â†’ false (Provides is last)
- `isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()` â†’ true (JvmStatic precedes)
- `isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()` â†’ for the test case where annotation is on separate line, this is false

So the condition `(node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())` is false when annotation is on separate line. No violation.

But what happens when `@[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo()` is on the same line?
- For `Provides`: `isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()` â†’ true, `isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()` â†’ true
- This enters `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`:
  - `isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList` â†’ false
  - `isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine` â†’ false (they're all on the same line)
  - Both false â†’ no violation from this check

Then:
- `node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION` â†’ false (parent IS ANNOTATION)
- So the `checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct` check is skipped.

So the `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes inside `@[...]` on the same line as the construct won't emit violations on their own. Only the `visitAnnotation` method I added will catch this. 

Now let me also think about what happens with `@[JvmStatic Provides]` on the same line + with `@Bar` before it on a separate line:

```kotlin
@Bar
@[JvmStatic Provides] fun foo()
```

For `Provides` inside `@[...]`:
- `isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()` â†’ true (JvmStatic)
- `isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()` â†’ true
- Goes to `checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine`:
  - `isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine` â†’ false (JvmStatic is on the same line)
  - No violation

For the `ANNOTATION` node (`@[JvmStatic Provides]`):
- `isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()` â†’ true
- Emits violation âś“

This seems correct. The fix is clean and targeted.

content.text:
The implementation looks correct. Let me summarize the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
Issue #1765: Array syntax annotations (`@[...]`) on the same line as the annotated construct were not being detected and flagged by the `AnnotationRule`.

### Root Cause
The `AnnotationRule` only visited `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes in its `beforeVisitChildNodes` method. When annotations use the array syntax (`@[...]`), the AST creates an `ANNOTATION` node that wraps multiple `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` children. The existing checks on `ANNOTATION_ENTRY` nodes inside `@[...]` would not trigger violations because:
1. The check for "annotation with parameter" wouldn't match (the entries inside `@[...]` typically don't have value argument lists)
2. The check for "multiple annotations on same line as construct" was explicitly skipped for entries with `treeParent.elementType == ANNOTATION`

### Fix
Added a new `visitAnnotation` method that handles `ANNOTATION` element type (the `@[...]` syntax). This method:
1. Checks if the `ANNOTATION` node is on the same line as the annotated construct
2. If so, emits a lint violation and auto-corrects by inserting a newline with proper indentation after the annotation

### Files Changed
1. **`AnnotationRule.kt`**: Added `ANNOTATION` case to the `when` block in `beforeVisitChildNodes`, and added the `visitAnnotation` private method
2. **`CHANGELOG.md`**: Added entry documenting the fix for issue #1765

1â†’# Changelog

11â†’### Fixed
12â†’* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
13â†’* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))

19â†’
20â†’### Added
21â†’
22â†’### Removed
23â†’
24â†’### Fixed
25â†’
26â†’* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
27â†’* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
28â†’* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
29â†’* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
30â†’* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.
31â†’* Rules provided via a custom rule set JAR (Ktlint CLI) or by an API provider are enabled by default. Only rules in the `experimental` rule set are disabled by default. ([#1747](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1747))

37â†’## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
38â†’
39â†’### Indent rule
40â†’
41â†’The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
42â†’
43â†’### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
44â†’
45â†’In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
46â†’
47â†’The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
48â†’
